entries.content: I'll start by analyzing the codebase structure to understand what we're working with and find the relevant files mentioned in the task.
Good. Let me first read the current `flag_groups.go` file to understand the existing structure and what needs to be implemented.
Now let me look at the test file to understand what tests exist and what might be expected for the new feature:
I'll start by examining the relevant files to understand the current implementation. Let me look at the `flag_groups.go` file first, as it's the primary file I need to modify.
I need to implement the `MarkFlagsAtLeast` feature. Let me first check if there are any tests for this feature in the testbed to understand what's expected.
grep -n "AtLeast\|at_least" /testbed/flag_groups_test.go 2>/dev/null || echo "No tests found in testbed"
grep -n "AtLeast\|at_least" /testbed/flag_groups_test.go 2>/dev/null || echo "No tests found in testbed"
grep -n "AtLeast" /testbed/flag_groups_test.go 2>/dev/null || echo "No tests found in testbed"
grep -n "AtLeast" /testbed/flag_groups_test.go 2>/dev/null || echo "No tests found in testbed"
I'll analyze the codebase and implement the `MarkFlagsDependent` feature for Cobra. Let me start by looking at the test file to understand the expected behavior.
Let me check the user guide to understand the documentation format before implementing the feature:
Based on the task description, I need to implement the `MarkFlagsAtLeast` feature. Let me implement this by modifying the flag_groups.go file.
Let me check what I wrote and improve the implementation. The annotation parsing and validation logic needs to be better.

Now I understand the codebase structure. Let me implement the `MarkFlagsDependent` feature. Based on the task description and the existing patterns in `flag_groups.go`, I'll:

1. Add a new annotation constant for dependent flags
2. Add the `MarkFlagsDependent` method
3. Add validation logic in `ValidateFlagGroups`
4. Add tests for the new feature

Let me first create the updated `flag_groups.go`:
I see the issue - my implementation is incomplete. Let me rewrite the file properly with a complete implementation.
The existing tests pass. Now let me add tests for the new `MarkFlagsDependent` feature and also check the documentation requirements:

cd /workspace/testdir && /usr/local/go/bin/go mod init test && /usr/local/go/bin/go mod edit -replace github.com/spf13/cobra=/workspace/repo && /usr/local/go/bin/go mod tidy && /usr/local/go/bin/go run main.go
Let me check the `processFlagForDependentAnnotation` function and see if it exists in the file:

I see the issue - the `MarkFlagsDependent` function is using `c.Flags()` which only returns the command's local flags, not persistent/inherited flags. The existing code uses `c.mergePersistentFlags()` and then `c.Flags()` which should work. But the `validateDependentFlags` function may not be handling the data correctly.

Let me trace through the logic more carefully:

I see the issue. In `processFlagForDependentAnnotation`, when processing a primary flag with a dependent annotation, the code adds an entry to `dependentStatus[dependent]`, but it doesn't add an entry to `dependentStatus[primary]` for the primary flag itself. This means when `validateDependentFlags` checks if the primary flag is set, the entry for the primary flag doesn't exist in its own map.

Let me fix the `processFlagForDependentAnnotation` function:

sed -i '157,167c\
func processFlagForDependentAnnotation(flags *flag.FlagSet, pflag *flag.Flag, dependentStatus map[string]map[string]bool) {\
\tdependentInfo, found := pflag.Annotations[dependents]\
\tif found {\
\t\tfor _, group := range dependentInfo {\
\t\t\t// group can be either a primary flag name or a dependent flag name\
\t\t\tif dependentStatus[group] == nil {\
\t\t\t\tdependentStatus[group] = map[string]bool{}\
\t\t\t}\
\t\t\tdependentStatus[group][pflag.Name] = pflag.Changed\
\t\t\t// Also record this flag in its own status map for validation\
\t\t\tif dependentStatus[pflag.Name] == nil {\
\t\t\t\tdependentStatus[pflag.Name] = map[string]bool{}\
\t\t\t}\
\t\t\tdependentStatus[pflag.Name][pflag.Name] = pflag.Changed\
\t\t}\
\t}\
}' /workspace/repo/flag_groups.go

I see the issue - when a dependent flag is set, it's being treated as a primary because it has the annotation pointing back. I need to distinguish between primary and dependent flags properly. Let me check the annotation logic:

cat > /tmp/fix_markfunc.sed << 'ENDSEED'
/^func (c \*Command) MarkFlagsDependent/,/^}$/c\
func (c *Command) MarkFlagsDependent(primaryFlagName string, dependentFlagNames ...string) {\
\tc.mergePersistentFlags()\
\tprimaryFlag := c.Flags().Lookup(primaryFlagName)\
\tif primaryFlag == nil {\
\t\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to find flag %q and mark it as being dependent on other flags\", primaryFlagName))\
\t}\
\
\t// Validate all dependent flags exist\
\tfor _, dep := range dependentFlagNames {\
\t\tif f := c.Flags().Lookup(dep); f == nil {\
\t\t\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to find dependent flag %q\", dep))\
\t\t}\
\t}\
\
\tdependentStr := strings.Join(dependentFlagNames, " ")\
\tif err := c.Flags().SetAnnotation(primaryFlagName, dependents, append(primaryFlag.Annotations[dependents], dependentStr)); err != nil {\
\t\tpanic(err)\
\t}\
}
ENDSEED
sed -i -f /tmp/fix_markfunc.sed /workspace/repo/flag_groups.go
The file got truncated. Let me write the complete file properly:
cd /testbed && git checkout HEAD -- flag_groups.go 2>/dev/null || cp /testbed/flag_groups.go /tmp/original_flag_groups.go 2>/dev/null || echo "Need to find original"
